perturbation.original_text: 2. Definition. "Confidential Information" means any information, ---------- technical data, or know-how, including, but not limited to that which relates to research, products, software, services, development, inventions, processes, designs, drawings, formulas, engineering, marketing, finances, financial models, and business plans, which Confidential Information is designated in writing to be confidential or proprietary, or if given orally, is confirmed promptly in writing as having been disclosed as confidential or proprietary. Confidential Information does not include information, technical data, or know-how (i) is in the possession of the receiving party at the time of disclosures shown by the receiving party's files and records immediately prior to the time of disclosure; or (ii) prior to or after the time of disclosure becomes part of the public knowledge or literature, not as a result of any inaction or action of the receiving party, or (iii) is required by law to be disclosed by the receiving party; (iv) is independently developed by the receiving party without utilization of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Definition. "Confidential Information" means information related to business plans. Confidential Information does not include information required by law to be disclosed by the receiving party.
perturbation.explanation: By significantly narrowing the definition of 'Confidential Information' to only information related to business plans and removing most of the exclusion clauses, it creates an in-text contradiction because the agreement refers to broader types of confidential information throughout, such as technical data, know-how, research, products, software, etc., when discussing non-disclosure obligations. The removal of exclusion (i), (ii) and (iv) which defined Confidential Information creates an in-text contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information. Each party agrees ------------------------------------------ not to use the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the other party for its own use or for any purpose except to carry out discussions concerning the completion of any business relationship between the two. Each party will not disclose the Confidential Information of the other party to third parties or to its employees except employees who are required to have the information in order to carry out the contemplated business. Each party will have employees to whom Confidential Information of the other party is disclosed sign a Non-Disclosure Agreement in content substantially similar to this agreement if such persons have not already signed such agreements obligating them to hold the Confidential Information in confidence. Each party agrees that it will take all reasonable steps to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of Confidential Information of the other party on order to prevent of unauthorized falling into the public domain or the possession of unauthorized persons. Each agrees to immediately notify the other party in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of such Confidential Information of the other party which may come to its attention.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'Confidential Information' to only information related to business plans, removing most of the exclusion clauses. The contradicted text (section 3) refers to a broader understanding of Confidential Information and implies that the information includes information beyond business plans, such as technical data, know-how, research, products, software, etc. The change creates a direct conflict because the non-disclosure obligations outlined in section 3 are now ambiguous and difficult to enforce with the significantly reduced definition of 'Confidential Information' provided in the changed text, because section 3 is referencing more than just information related to business plans, and that is the only definition provided.

perturbation.original_text: Each party will have employees to whom Confidential Information of the other party is disclosed sign a Non-Disclosure Agreement in content substantially similar to this agreement if such persons have not already signed such agreements obligating them to hold the Confidential Information in confidence.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party will encourage employees to whom Confidential Information is disclosed to maintain confidentiality.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates employees sign a Non-Disclosure Agreement. The modified text only encourages confidentiality, creating an in-text contradiction within Section 3. This creates uncertainty about the enforceability of confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Non-Disclosure of Confidential Information. Each party agrees ------------------------------------------ not to use the Confidential Information disclosed to it by the other party for its own use or for any purpose except to carry out discussions concerning the completion of any business relationship between the two. Each party will not disclose the Confidential Information of the other party to third parties or to its employees except employees who are required to have the information in order to carry out the contemplated business. Each party will encourage employees to whom Confidential Information is disclosed to maintain confidentiality. Each party agrees that it will take all reasonable steps to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of Confidential Information of the other party on order to prevent of unauthorized falling into the public domain or the possession of unauthorized persons. Each agrees to immediately notify the other party in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of such Confidential Information of the other party which may come to its attention.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated that employees sign a Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA), but the changed text only encourages employees to maintain confidentiality. This directly contradicts the implicit expectation of a signed NDA within the contradicted section. The original NDA requirement ensures a legally binding obligation, while the encouragement to maintain confidentiality is not a legally binding obligation. Therefore, the altered language creates a contradiction making obligations unclear, leading to a strong in-text contradiction based on Omissions - In Text Contradiction as per the definitions provided.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Term. The term of this Agreement shall be five (5) years.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Term. The term of this Agreement shall be automatically renewed unless either party objects.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the fixed five-year term to automatic renewal creates an uncertainty. If the original fixed term is assumed in other sections of the agreement (for example, calculations of liability or expected project duration), the new renewal clause creates an in-text contradiction regarding the expected duration of the agreement and obligations. It specifically makes the text contradictory in section 6, when regarding the successors of each party
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. General Provisions. This Agreement will be governed by the ------------------ laws of the State of California. This Agreement will be binding upon the successors of each party, and will be for the benefit of each party, its successors, and its assigns. Each party agrees that it would be difficult to measure the damage to such party from the breach of the other party's obligations hereunder, that injury to such party from any such breach would be impossible to calculate, and that monetary damages would therefor be an inadequate remedy; accordingly, each party agrees that the other party shall be entitled, in addition to all other remedies it might have, to injunctions or other appropriate orders to restrain any such breach without showing or proving any actual damage. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this Agreement as of the date and year written above.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a 5-year term, while the changed text introduces automatic renewal. This contradicts section 6, which assumes a fixed term when stating the agreement will be binding upon the successors of each party and for their benefit. The automatic renewal makes the duration uncertain, which creates a contradiction regarding the expected duration of the agreement and obligations. Because it cannot be determined how long this agreement will last, the benefit to successors is indeterminable, and this creates a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees that it would be difficult to measure the damage to such party from the breach of the other party's obligations hereunder, that injury to such party from any such breach would be impossible to calculate, and that monetary damages would therefor be an inadequate remedy; accordingly, each party agrees that the other party shall be entitled, in addition to all other remedies it might have, to injunctions or other appropriate orders to restrain any such breach without showing or proving any actual damage.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees that monetary damages shall be the primary remedy for any breach of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that monetary damages are an inadequate remedy. The change directly contradicts this by stating monetary damages are the primary remedy. This creates a fundamental in-text contradiction about the intended remedies for a breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. General Provisions. This Agreement will be governed by the ------------------ laws of the State of California. This Agreement will be binding upon the successors of each party, and will be for the benefit of each party, its successors, and its assigns. Each party agrees that monetary damages shall be the primary remedy for any breach of this agreement. IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have executed this Agreement as of the date and year written above.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that monetary damages are an inadequate remedy, while the changed text specifies that monetary damages 'shall be the primary remedy'. The contradicted text also states monetary damages 'shall be the primary remedy'. This directly conflicts with the original text and supports the changed text which is a direct in-text contradiction.
